title: National Constitutional Carry Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
plain_summary: This bill would create a national law that stops states and cities from making rules against carrying guns in public places. The bill says that blocking people from carrying firearms goes against their Second Amendment rights. Under this law, states and local governments could not punish or restrict law-abiding citizens who want to carry guns in public. The bill would make it so people can carry firearms without getting special permission from their state or city.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the National Constitutional Carry Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress has found several important facts. The Second Amendment to the Constitution guarantees that American citizens have the individual right to keep and bear arms, including carrying arms in public for self-defense. This amendment says these rights shall not be infringed, meaning they cannot be limited or taken away. The amendment was written to preserve the security of a free state. The Supreme Court has ruled in several cases that confirm these rights. In two thousand eight, the Court said the Second Amendment gives individuals the right to keep and bear arms. In two thousand ten, the Court ruled that the Fourteenth Amendment makes the Second Amendment apply to all states. In two thousand twenty-two, the Court said the Second and Fourteenth Amendments protect the right to carry arms outside the home for self-defense. The Court also said that any laws limiting these rights must be consistent with this nation's historical tradition of firearm regulation. Some states and local governments have passed gun control laws that do not match the text of the Second Amendment or the nation's historical tradition of firearm regulation. Making it a crime to peacefully carry firearms in public goes against the original meaning of the Second Amendment. Any state or local restriction on the right to keep and bear arms hurts the Second Amendment's stated purpose of ensuring the security of a free state.

sections.title: Section 3 The Right to Keep and Bear Arms
sections.text: This section changes an existing law in the United States Code. The new law says that no state or local government may impose criminal or civil penalties on carrying firearms in public. This applies to both residents and nonresidents of that state who are United States citizens and are otherwise allowed to possess firearms under state and federal law. States and local governments also cannot indirectly limit carrying firearms, including by creating financial or other barriers. Any state or local law, regulation, custom, or practice that makes carrying firearms in public a crime, creates penalties, or otherwise discourages carrying firearms shall have no force or effect. This applies to any resident or nonresident who is a United States citizen and otherwise eligible to possess firearms under state and federal law. The term state includes the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and the possessions of the United States but not including the Canal Zone. The term public includes any place held open to the public, regardless of who owns it. However, for privately-owned locations open to the public, it does not include places where the owner clearly and conspicuously communicates a prohibition of firearms on the premises. Public also does not include places where screening for firearms is conducted under state law.
